title: Construction Mortgages — Self-Build & Custom Home Financing
metaTitle: Construction Mortgage in BC — Custom Home Financing
metaDescription: Construction mortgage financing in Greater Vancouver for self-builds, custom homes and major teardown-and-rebuilds. Draw schedules, completion mortgages and rates explained.
intro: Building a custom home — or doing a major teardown-and-rebuild — runs on a different mortgage product. Funds advance in draws as work progresses, and the file converts to a permanent mortgage at completion.

body.heading: How a draw mortgage works
paragraphs: A construction or 'draw' mortgage advances funds in stages tied to specific milestones — typically: foundation complete (20–25% of loan), framing/lockup (30–40%), interior finish (60–70%), and occupancy (final draw). Each draw is preceded by an inspection or appraisal confirming the work is done.

Interest accrues on advanced funds only — so early in the build your interest cost is small. By completion, you're paying interest on the full balance. Most construction mortgages are interest-only during the build phase and convert to principal-and-interest at occupancy.

body.heading: Cost and qualifying
paragraphs: Construction mortgages are typically 0.5–1.5% above standard residential rates because of the higher risk during the build. Down payment requirements are usually 20–30% of the projected as-completed value. You qualify based on your post-completion mortgage payment, not the construction-phase interest-only payment.

Documentation includes: full architectural plans, building permit, fixed-price construction contract from a licensed builder (or detailed budget for owner-builders), and a cost-to-complete projection from a qualified appraiser.

whoItsFor: Are building a custom home in Greater Vancouver
Have a fixed-price contract with a licensed builder (or own-build experience)
requirements: Architectural plans and city building permit
Land already owned (or being purchased simultaneously)

faqs.question: Can I build it myself?
faqs.answer: Owner-builder construction mortgages exist but are tougher to qualify for and require more documentation — detailed itemized budgets, contractor breakdowns for any sub-trades, and proof of construction experience. Some lenders won't fund owner-builds at all.

faqs.question: How long can construction take?
faqs.answer: Most lenders give a 12-month build window; some go to 18–24 months. Going past the window without a meaningful reason can trigger penalty rate or default.

faqs.question: What if costs go over budget?
faqs.answer: You're responsible for cost overruns. The lender funds against the original committed budget; overages come from your pocket or a separate financing arrangement. Build a 10–15% contingency into your initial budget.
